titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 581

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=904 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 16:12:05.641928+00 | translation moa=905 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 16:12:05.652949+00

p.cl:
M Rex Scott’ . Iusticiis . Vicecomitibus . Prepositis . Ministris . Cunctisque aliis probis hominibus totius terre sue
tam futuris quam presentibus salutem .

Sciatis me concessisse deo et Sancto Cuthberto De Collingham et Monachis
ibidem deo seruientibus  ut ipsi et eorum seruientibus per totam terram meam sint liberi et quieti ab omni te
loneo de propriis catallis et mercaturis eorum . Prohibeo itaque ut nemo contra hanc libertatem eis infra
terram meam uim aut iniuriam faciat super forisfacturam meam .

Testibus . Nich’ Camerario . Ricardo Capellano . Regis .
Ricardo de Sancto Albano clerico Regis .
Malcolm, king of the Scots, to justices, sheriffs, provosts, officials, and all other worthy men of his entire land, as much future as present, greeting.

Know me to have conceded to God, to Saint Cuthbert of Coldingham, and to the monks serving God there, that they and their tenants throughout my entire land be free and quit from all toll on their own cattle and merchandise. I therefore forbid that anyone do violence or injury to them within my land contrary to this liberty on pain of my forfeiture.
As witnesses: Nicholas the chamberlain, Richard the king's chaplain, Richard of St Albans the king's clerk.
